steps.title: Identify what wins over Edward's heart
steps.content: The passage states: “This affectionate willingness to fall in with his own plans quite won the old man's heart.” This sentence is the passage's turning point—it explains the direct cause of Edward's warmth toward Nathaniel.

steps.title: Match this cause to the answer choices
steps.content:
The matching choice identifies the passage's main purpose: how Nathaniel's qualities lead to closeness.

The others miss this core dynamic, instead focusing on peripheral details—logistics of sleeping on the ship, excitement for the journey, or fabricating initial wariness that contradicts the “auspiciously begun” opening.

That matches A.
